summary: Backend-only testing for 15-bug fix batch. All 8 specified scenarios tested and passing after fixing one critical bug found during testing.

critical.issue: NameError: `profile` variable referenced but never defined in create_log_entry(). The cache invalidation block `if profile:` should not have a guard — `profile` was never fetched in this function. Caused 500 Internal Server Error on every POST /api/log call.
critical.status: FIXED by T1 — removed `if profile:` guard, cache invalidation now always runs

action_items: No remaining action items — all tests pass after the `if profile:` fix.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 826 (now fixed): `if profile:` used undefined variable — always audit cache invalidation code after refactoring.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (line 826: removed `if profile:` guard from POST /api/log cache invalidation)
seed_data_creation: One TEST_ log entry created and deleted during test teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 8 bug-fix scenarios verified: (1) weekStats date-range query works; (2) weekStats legacy week-number fallback works; (3) today session returns currentWeek integer; (4) weekly-review caches with weekStart field; (5) weekly-review second call returns cached=True; (6) POST /api/log no longer crashes (NameError fixed); (7) weekly-review regenerates after log post; (8) coach/chat is user-scoped; (9) apply-recommendation uses userId-scoped profile.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: E1 refactored cache invalidation in POST /api/log to use weekStart key but accidentally introduced `if profile:` guard without fetching profile first. The variable `profile` was never defined in the `create_log_entry` function scope, causing NameError → 500 on every workout log creation. Fix: remove the guard entirely — weekStart can be computed from datetime without needing a profile lookup.
